text.p:
3 Masakin El Rimaya - El Haram - Giza
E-mail: Hithamab@Hotmail.com
Phone: 012-2857519
Hitham M. Abo Bakr

div.p: Objective
Computer science is my field of work. My aim is to work in an appropriate level in either computer system developing branch, or research and development branch.
Professional Experience
21 Aug. 1999 – until now Giza Systems Engineering Cairo
Job Title: Technology Expert Developer
Design and implementation the Text Analysis System for Arabic and English language using Morphological system (MoRpH) developed during preparing my Ph.D.
Building Notification system for sending SMS using gateways, or via SMPP for Egypt Air flow.

Worked in team to develop “FaxStudio” application. My role is design and implement for a Fax Server that deals with Dialogic cards for sending requested faxes using VC++ and ATL and DCOM technologies. Design and implement a Client-server Utility to monitor fax lines remotely using Sockets.

Worked in team to develop “@nywhere” application This application notifies Mail clients with Short Message Service (SMS) when receiving Email. Client can reply using another SMS or using IVR system. We develop this application using Visual C++. My role in the project was: Designing and implementing the NT service that manage sending and receiving the SMS messages from SMS gateway and sends it via Winsock to Clients that handle the Messages. Designing and implementing the Client Program that request the SMS, configuration Program for this project and monitor utility.
Design a program to generate ring tones and logos for Nokia mobiles using smart messaging specifications and send it using SMS service.

Worked in team to develop a customizable Interactive voice Response (IVR) system called “IVR Studio ”using visual c++ and the tapi library, 1999/2000. My role in the project was: Designing and implementing the graphical user interface, designing and implementing some of the core functions and designing and implementing the client monitor program and report generator program part in the project.
Design a video streaming system and design a homepage that gets the online video streams and a video on demand depending on a user privilege.
Building homepage for Ministry of Education for “EL Thanawaya”, and “El Tanseek” for year 2000.
20 Aug. 1995 – 20 Aug. 1999 Saudisoft Egypt Branch Cairo.
Job Title: Senior Developer + Network Administrator in Saudisoft Co.
Network administrator for Saudisoft Co. (80 Users) Installing and configuring NT servers, Proxy, Exchange Server 5.5, and IIS 4.0.
Job Title : System Programmer
Putting a design and implementing it for Arabic text contextual analysis. I.e. doing the text layout and shaping. We used this engine in the developing the OTC line printer driver for Windows 95
Building device drivers for Windows 3.11 that driver is used in the “MDOS “project.
Building virtual device drivers for Windows 95.

Working in a many types of VxDs under Windows 95 and build the device drivers for "Waseet Saudisoft" project. In Waseet project we made the Arabization engine to the DOS and solve the problem of the printing in the Arabic Dos under Windows95. Make a generic device driver that port the printed data from the Dos Applications to windows applications with correct format to the tables in the printed data.
Building Arabic printer device derives for line printers under windows 95
Writing an application that converts the captured printed data from the DOS and then converts that data to the RTF format.
Develop an application that offer a security on the files and folders on a PC for the owner to the PC and hide it from other users (for DOS and Windows 95).
Building virtual device drivers for windows NT 4.0. Working mainly in VDD.
Building API For Dos developers working with Dbase, C, Basic, Clipper, and Clarion.
Building utilities used for generating Arabic Open True Type Fonts files.
Adding Arabization module to BRS 32-bit Toolkit.
Additional Professional Activities
Building software protection, and Using hardware protection to protect projects.
Debugging & Back-engineering. Hooking Windows APIs. Install and configure Dialogic cards.
Education
Preparing for Ph.D. “Building Algorithms and Databases for Arabic Linguistic Analysis”
1996 - 1999 Cairo University – Faculty of Engineering.
Master's Degree of Engineering in Computer science.
M. Sc. “Code Generation and Optimization for VLIW Machines”.
1990 – 1995. Faculty of Engineering.
B.Sc. of Engineering in Electronics & communication.
Degree: Very Good with honor degree (83.03 %).
Degree of project: Excellent.
Diploma Courses Studied
Parallel and Distributed Computer Architectures- Computer language and compiler – ATM - AI – Computer Simulation - Adv. Computer Graphics – Advanced Computer Networks – Neural Networks.
Training Courses
Microsoft Certified Solution Developer Courses, Knowlodg Nov. 2000
Digital Unix Administration & Management , GSE Oct.1999
WINDOWS NT Server 4.0, IBM Egypt (MCP) Oct.1998
OLE2, Microsoft Jan.1996
